discovery.text: Recommended via Concierge Chat for Boston late-summer trip

narrative.summary: America's longest continuously operating hotel, open since 1855. Birthplace of Boston cream pie and the Parker House roll. Malcolm X and Ho Chi Minh both worked here. Dead centre on the Freedom Trail, steps from everything historical.

blocks.title: The Place
blocks.body: The Parker House has been running since 1855 — it's the kind of hotel where you can feel the weight of the place. The list of former residents and workers is genuinely absurd: Malcolm X worked here as a busboy, Ho Chi Minh as a baker, Charles Dickens read A Christmas Carol here for its American premiere. The Boston cream pie was invented in the kitchen downstairs. It's a grand old dame that still cleans up well.

blocks.title: Location
blocks.body: Dead centre of everything historical — literally on the Freedom Trail, steps from the Old South Meeting House, King's Chapel, and Faneuil Hall. Government Center T station is a minute away. This is the base if the historical sites are the priority.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Anytime — it's a year-round institution. Late August for summer weather and outdoor dining nearby. Order the Boston cream pie in the dining room at least once.

_hotel.bestFor: History lovers, central location, classic experience
